content:
# Van Setup and Teardown Checklists

**Tool ID:** T205-01
**Block:** B205 (Coffee Van Operations)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Owner
**Time Required:** 30–45 minutes (setup); 20–30 minutes (teardown)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

A coffee van is a cafe that assembles itself fresh every morning and packs itself away every night. Miss one step in setup and you discover it when the first customer is standing in front of you — no water connected, no beans loaded, generator not running. Miss one step in teardown and you arrive the next morning to spilt milk in the van, a dead battery, or health and safety violations.

This checklist is your insurance policy. Follow it completely, every time, no exceptions.

**Built for Owner working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Comprehensive setup and teardown checklists for mobile coffee van operations. Covers vehicle preparation, utility connections (water, power, gas), equipment setup, stock loading, service readiness, and secure pack-down procedures. Designed for a self-contained mobile operation that must be fully functional within 30–45 minutes of arriving at a location.

This tool supports **Coffee Van Operations** (B205) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Owner (or van operator)
**Time Required:** 30–45 minutes setup; 20–30 minutes teardown
**Frequency:** Every trading day or event setup.

**What You'll Need:**
- This checklist (laminated and kept in the van at all times)
- Vehicle keys, generator, water containers, LPG (if applicable)
- All equipment pre-loaded or accessible
- Whiteboard marker for laminated version

**Steps:**
1. Arrive at location with sufficient time for full setup before first customer
2. Work through the Setup checklist in sequence
3. Do not serve until every box is ticked
4. At end of trading, work through the Teardown checklist in sequence
5. Do not drive away until every box is ticked

---

## Checklist

### SETUP CHECKLIST

#### Date: ________ | Location: ________ | Event: ________ | Start Time: ________

#### 1. Vehicle & Positioning (5 minutes)

- [ ] Vehicle parked level on firm ground (use levelling blocks if needed)
- [ ] Handbrake engaged, engine off, wheel chocks in place
- [ ] Service hatch faces customer traffic / agreed position
- [ ] Distance from neighbouring vendors adequate (check event requirements)
- [ ] Vehicle registration and insurance visible (if required by event or council)
- [ ] Fire extinguisher accessible and in-date
- [ ] First aid kit accessible

#### 2. Power (5 minutes)

- [ ] Generator positioned safely: minimum 1m from van, downwind, on level ground
- [ ] Fuel level checked: _____ (full / 3/4 / 1/2 — sufficient for trading hours?)
- [ ] Generator started and running stable
- [ ] Electrical connections made: espresso machine, grinder, fridge, lights
- [ ] RCD / safety trip tested (press test button, confirm it trips, reset)
- [ ] All cables routed safely — no trip hazards, no water contact risk
- [ ] IF mains power provided by event: confirm supply, test before connecting equipment

#### 3. Water (5 minutes)

- [ ] Fresh water tank filled: _____ L (sufficient for day? 1L per 4–5 drinks approx)
- [ ] Water pump on and primed — run tap to confirm flow
- [ ] Waste water tank empty: _____ % full (must start < 10%)
- [ ] Water connections tight — no leaks at joins
- [ ] Water filter attached (if applicable)
- [ ] Hot water boiler connected and heating (if separate from espresso machine)

#### 4. Equipment Setup (10 minutes)

- [ ] Espresso machine turned on — allow 15–20 min warm-up
- [ ] Machine secure on counter — will not slide during serving
- [ ] Grinder positioned and secured — hopper lid on
- [ ] Beans loaded: house blend _____ kg | decaf _____ kg
- [ ] Grinder dialled in: 2–3 test shots pulled (18g/36g/25–28s)
- [ ] Grind setting recorded: _____
- [ ] Knock box positioned and accessible
- [ ] Blender secured (if applicable)
- [ ] Under-counter or portable fridge running, temperature: _____ C (target 1–5 C)
- [ ] POS / payment terminal on, charged, signal confirmed (check mobile data)
- [ ] Card reader connected and test transaction approved

#### 5. Stock Loading (5 minutes)

- [ ] Milk loaded into fridge: whole ___L | oat ___L | soy ___L
- [ ] Takeaway cups stocked: 8oz ___ | 12oz ___ | 16oz ___
- [ ] Lids stocked: flat ___ | dome ___
- [ ] Sleeves / holders available
- [ ] Syrups positioned at bar: vanilla, caramel, hazelnut (check levels)
- [ ] Hot chocolate / chai powder stocked
- [ ] Sugar, sweetener, stirrers available
- [ ] Napkins stocked
- [ ] Pastries / food loaded into display (if applicable)
- [ ] Pastry count recorded for waste tracking: _____ pcs

#### 6. Service Area Setup (5 minutes)

- [ ] Service counter clean and clear
- [ ] Menu board / chalkboard displayed with prices
- [ ] Price list visible and accurate
- [ ] A-frame / signage placed to attract customers
- [ ] Sugar/stirrer station set up for customer self-service
- [ ] Waste bin positioned (customer-facing if applicable)
- [ ] Cash float loaded: EUR _____ (coins and small notes)
- [ ] Sanitiser and cloth at station
- [ ] Allergen information available (14 EU allergens)

#### 7. Final Pre-Service Check

- [ ] All setup items completed above
- [ ] Test espresso: taste, flow, crema — acceptable quality?
- [ ] Test milk steam: temperature 60–65 C?
- [ ] Service hatch open / awning deployed
- [ ] Team briefing (if 2+ operators): roles, signals, end time
- [ ] Ready to serve first customer

---

### TEARDOWN CHECKLIST

#### Trading Over: ________ | Final Transaction: ________

#### 1. Last Customer & Sales Close (5 minutes)

- [ ] Last customer served, service hatch notice displayed
- [ ] POS end-of-day: Revenue EUR _____ | Transactions: _____
- [ ] Cash counted and reconciled: EUR _____ (variance: _____)
- [ ] Cash secured
- [ ] Card terminal closed, receipt printed if needed

#### 2. Waste & Stock (3 minutes)

- [ ] Unsold food/pastries counted and logged: _____ pcs = EUR _____
- [ ] Remaining stock noted: beans ___kg, milk ___L, cups 8oz: ___, 12oz: ___
- [ ] Perishables stored in cool bag or fridge for transport
- [ ] Non-perishable stock secured for transit

#### 3. Equipment Cleaning (10 minutes)

- [ ] Espresso machine: backflush with detergent, flush group heads
- [ ] Portafilters: remove baskets, rinse, reassemble
- [ ] Steam wand: soak tip, wipe, purge
- [ ] Drip tray: empty and clean
- [ ] Machine exterior wiped down
- [ ] Grinders: empty hoppers into sealed bags, brush out retained grounds
- [ ] Knock box emptied and cleaned
- [ ] Blender: disassemble, rinse, store (if applicable)
- [ ] All surfaces sanitised
- [ ] Milk jugs washed
- [ ] Counter wiped down (inside and service-facing)
- [ ] Floor swept

#### 4. Utilities Disconnection (5 minutes)

- [ ] Espresso machine powered off
- [ ] Water pump off
- [ ] Remaining fresh water level noted: _____ L
- [ ] Waste water level noted: _____ % (empty if > 70% or if time to next service is > 24 hours)
- [ ] IF waste water to drain: dispose responsibly (not on grass, not in storm drains)
- [ ] All electrical connections disconnected
- [ ] Generator off, cooled down, fuel cap secured
- [ ] Generator stored securely
- [ ] LPG turned off at cylinder (if applicable)

#### 5. Pack Down & Secure (5 minutes)

- [ ] All equipment secured for transit — nothing loose that could slide or fall
- [ ] Fridge door secured shut
- [ ] Service hatch closed and locked
- [ ] Awning retracted and secured
- [ ] A-frame / signage packed away
- [ ] Rubbish removed from site (leave no trace)
- [ ] Van interior tidy — nothing on the floor
- [ ] External van check: lights working, mirrors clean, tyre pressure visual check

#### 6. Departure

- [ ] Wheel chocks removed
- [ ] Area checked: nothing left behind at the pitch
- [ ] Van started, all systems normal
- [ ] Drive away checklist: lights, indicators, mirrors adjusted

#### Handover / Notes for Next Session

| Item | Notes |
|------|-------|
| Stock needed | *e.g., Need 6L oat milk, 2 sleeves 12oz cups* |
| Equipment issues | *e.g., Generator pull-cord stiff — service needed* |
| Location notes | *e.g., Pitch 3 at market is better — more foot traffic* |
| Other | |

#### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Time | Signature |
|------|------|------|-----------|
| Setup completed | | | |
| Teardown completed | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Generator Failure During Service

**Why it happens:** Fuel runs out, generator overloaded, or engine fault. Everything stops — espresso machine, grinder, fridge, POS.
**How to prevent:** Check fuel before every service. Carry a spare fuel can. Know your generator's capacity vs your equipment's total draw. Service the generator per manufacturer schedule.
**If it's already happening:** Refuel immediately (engine off, cool down first). If engine fault, you cannot serve — pack down and go. Always carry a backup manual card reader for payments.

### Risk 2: Water Running Out Mid-Service

**Why it happens:** Underestimating how much water the espresso machine uses. A busy morning can use 60–80L easily.
**How to prevent:** Know your tank capacity and your typical usage per drink. Calculate: drinks expected x 0.2L per drink (espresso machine water usage). Carry spare water containers.
**If it's already happening:** Switch to takeaway-only to conserve water for drinks (no hand-washing except sanitiser). Refill from a safe water source if available.

### Risk 3: Leaving Equipment Unsecured During Transit

**Why it happens:** Rushing to pack down after a long day. Grinder left on counter, portafilter not clipped in, knock box of wet grounds left open.
**How to prevent:** The teardown checklist exists for this reason. The "secure for transit" section must be completed before driving. One sudden brake and an unsecured grinder becomes a dangerous projectile.
**If it's already happening:** Invest in securing straps, anti-slip mats, and dedicated storage positions for every piece of equipment. Nothing should be loose.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Market Trader:** Add pitch allocation and market rules compliance checks. Some markets require specific waste disposal, fire safety, and insurance evidence.
**Event Caterer:** Add event-specific sections: event organiser contact, power supply arrangements, start/finish times, estimated footfall.
**Permanent Pitch:** If you operate from the same spot daily, simplify the vehicle/positioning section. Focus on utility and equipment checks.
**Multi-Van Operation:** Add a vehicle identification row. Each van should have its own laminated copy. Include fleet maintenance schedule reference.

**Making it yours:**
- Laminate this checklist and keep it permanently in the van's cab
- Add photos of correct setup configuration for training new operators
- Include your generator make/model and servicing schedule
- Add your local council pitch permit number and conditions

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T205-02 — Location Performance Tracker (L2, tracker)
- T205-03 — Event Booking Management Kit (L2, template)
- T205-04 — Vehicle and Equipment Maintenance Schedule (L2, tracker)
- T205-05 — Licence and Compliance Register (L1, tracker)

**Related blocks:**

**Parent block:** B205 — Coffee Van Operations
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
